mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly reducing present equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, causing investors to price in lower corporate profits and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in > 55% of instances when UNRATE crosses the threshold, or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio falls below 0.1.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 20-day returns in > 60% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, triggering short-term positive momentum in equity markets over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 5-day returns during recovery phases, or mean forward return difference vs baseline is less than 0.05%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index levels above 280 indicate entrenched inflationary pressures that force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are non-negative when CPIAUCSL exceeds the threshold, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01 in high-inflation environments.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market expansion, boosting consumer spending expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to generate positive 60-day returns in > 50% of observations, or the interaction effect reverses sign during economic expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth directly increases corporate earnings capacity and economic activity, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show no statistically significant correlation with 60-day forward returns, or mean returns are negative during periods of positive GDP growth.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal contracting manufacturing activity and weakening economic momentum, prompting immediate equity market sell-offs and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in > 65% of cases following two consecutive INDPRO declines, or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark.
